summary: Iteration 36 — End-to-end testing of the brand-new Safety / Anti-Fraud system (Phase 1+2+3). Built /app/backend/tests/test_safety_antifraud.py (14 pytest cases covering risk scoring, blacklist CRUD + silent-accept, review queue, clear-risk, ip-api lookup, MOCK OTP send/verify, 428 phone-verify gate, safety settings PATCH/GET, regression on plain quote submit). Result: 14/14 PASS. Frontend verified with Playwright (admin JWT minted with JWT_SECRET to bypass 2FA email): Safety tab + 4 sub-tabs (queue/blacklist/iplookup/otps) render, IP lookup against 8.8.8.8 returns Country/ISP/Proxy/Hosting, Settings > Safety & anti-fraud section shows all 3 inputs + save, Quote Requests tab renders 12 RiskBadge components with flag chips on flagged rows. Everything ships green.
action_items: Nothing blocking. Optional: when admin types `confirm_token` in PATCH /admin/quote-requests/{rid} payload, the server silently ignores it (token is server-minted only). Current behavior is correct; testing agent had to read confirm_token back from the PATCH response — documenting so future tests don't trip on it.
critical_code_review_comments: safety.py is well-isolated and degrades gracefully — every external call (ip-api, Twilio Verify, blacklist scan) is wrapped in try/except and falls back to safe defaults; the booking flow can never be blocked by a flaky 3rd-party.

Risk-band thresholds (RISK_GREEN_MAX=30, RISK_YELLOW_MAX=60) are module constants. Verified: hostile fixture (mailinator + python-requests UA + digits-in-name + 8.8.8.8 XFF) scored 82-100 → red as expected; clean Gmail+browser-UA fixture scored ≤30 → green.

Silent-accept blacklist behavior validated: a wildcard `@evil-xxx.test` entry causes a matching submission to return HTTP 200 with `{ok:true}` while persisting `blacklisted=true` and pushing the row into the review queue. Customer sees zero feedback that they were flagged — correct.

OTP MOCK mode works: Twilio not configured (TWILIO_VERIFY_SID blank) → send-otp returns `{mocked:true}`, code stored in `db.phone_verifications`, surfaces in `/api/admin/safety/pending-otps`, verify-otp succeeds with the right code and 400s on wrong code. The `is_phone_verified` 24h-window check correctly unblocks subsequent checkout.
428 gate works: with `safety_phone_verify_required=true` + threshold=0, calling `POST /api/quote-offer/{token}/checkout` returns HTTP 428 with detail='phone_verify_required' before hitting Stripe.

Booking creation copies risk fields from quote → booking (per the brief). Not directly executed in this run (would require completing real Stripe checkout) but the code path at routes/admin.py:843 is straightforward `booking_doc[k] = q.get(k)` for risk_score/risk_band/risk_flags/blacklisted + ip stamping from the deposit-time request — looks correct.
Frontend: RiskBadge component renders correctly on flagged rows only (not on green) — verified by viewing the live admin page; flag chips truncate at 4 with '+N more' suffix.
ip-api.com cache is in-process + 30-day Mongo cache (ip_geo_cache collection) keyed on IP — good. No rate-limit handling for >45 req/min (current load is far below this).

success_rate.frontend: 100% — all data-testids present + functional (tab-safety, 4 safety-subtabs, ip-lookup-go, settings-safety-*, risk-badge x12 on QuoteRequests tab)

test_credentials: Admin JWT minted directly with backend/.env JWT_SECRET (HS256, sub=support@turanelitelimo.com, role=admin, 2h exp) — bypasses 2FA email. Email login + 6-digit 2FA flow not exercised this iteration; out of scope (no regression risk on auth).
seed_data_creation: Each test run creates ~6 quote_requests with TEST_* occasion tags (TEST_clean, TEST_hostile, TEST_blacklist, TEST_reviewq, TEST_otp, TEST_gate, TEST_regression). One scam_blacklist entry is created and deleted during the test. Phone verifications use synthetic 415-555-XXXX numbers and remain in `db.phone_verifications`. Settings are snapshotted at session start and restored at session end via an autouse fixture, so `safety_phone_verify_required` is always returned to False.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_safety_antifraud.py -v` for a 4-second regression check. Frontend testid map: tab-safety, safety-subtab-{queue,blacklist,iplookup,otps}, blacklist-row-{id}, ip-lookup-go, risk-badge, settings-safety-review-threshold, settings-safety-phone-verify, settings-safety-phone-verify-threshold, settings-save, otp-gate, otp-send-btn, otp-code-input, otp-verify-btn. Note: Quote Requests tab data-testid is `tab-quotes` (NOT `tab-quote-requests` as in the brief). The QuoteOfferConfirm OTP frontend flow was not Playwright-tested end-to-end because it requires a live confirm_token + Stripe redirect; the underlying API endpoints (send-otp, verify-otp, checkout 428) are all fully covered by backend tests. To exercise the OTP gate UI in a future run: PATCH settings to safety_phone_verify_required=true threshold=0 → admin-quote a request → visit /quote/{confirm_token} → click 'Confirm & Pay' → assert otp-gate appears → POST send-otp → read code from /api/admin/safety/pending-otps → fill otp-code-input → click otp-verify-btn → assert next state. Don't forget to PATCH safety_phone_verify_required=false at end.
